item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is bad. The interview is rambling, goes on for too long and basically is a complete retelling of the skips whole life. Other than that, the idea has been done before, even if this take is somewhat fresh. My main gripe with this piece is the English, basically, it's not well written. There's odd grammar, odd phrases and words, and odd ways of communicating that seem to be more related with the way the author wrote it than with any in-universe explanation. There are way too many fuzzy things going on for me to correct them, and maybe I would end up changing the article too much. So I would recommend you to do a full rewrite, maybe get some help from someone knowledgeable that can help you with the language. Until then, I'll downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sad to see a low rating, this is definitely my jam, though i understand the criticism - maybe you could separate the dialogue into different interviews instead of giving all the information in one place.
I do like how the interviewer is afraid of anything he might say that upsets 2681 (and may i suggest that you give him a name? I'd introduce in another dialogue, maybe with a psychiatrist?), and i think 2681 is aware of it, which gets him even more depressed. Definitely +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interview is rather weak, but it could be improved. -1 for now, but that's subject to change.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The author can choose to tag their own article, but they should have a clear idea on what they are tagging and what tags are needed. Otherwise, someone will get to it eventually.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you. I thought the user was supposed to add them in.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the info.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
That is indeed correct. Thus, I have removed the tags.

To the Author of the article, if you are not clear about tagging, let the tagging staff handle that. Note that untagged articles are usually targeted for tagging, and thus improperly-tagged articles will be out of the tag staff's radar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You should remove the tags and let the tag team take care of it instead, 2 of your tags don't exist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry for the late reply, Malik!

I understand. I think you should edit the SCP's personality, then. They can still be anxious and whatnot, but if he rambles about his life story to a stranger, then perhaps a more open personality would be more fitting?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Er, RubySlax, you can edit other people's articles for grammar issues without their permission. None of scrubbyrant's edits changed the content of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: scrubbyrant, I may not be a mod or admin, but I want to remind you not to edit other's work unless you they gave you the green light to do so, if the author did give you permission, then ignore this comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Nitpickings and the like
- Nitpicks begone

Looking over the idea as a whole, it isn't very interesting to me.
Now I say this because it just doesn't feel original, y'know? The idea of being able to influence animals, to my knowledge, is perhaps even common.
I feel that, given more revisions, there is opportunity for a far more original concept to be introduced. As stands, it does give off a Vermin God sort of vibe, and I feel it would be wise to try and ease away from that. I encourage you try to think of ways to make it more original, more captivating for your readers — how you could go about achieving this, I don't know. Best to ask around for that!

(Also, dear lord, the mental state of this poor person)

As for the interview logs, I find this person's story hard to really grasp or in fact even find it convincing, particularly due to the verbosity in their responses. Perhaps consider ways that you could cut down on the dialogue — it will certainly make it more convincing!
Particularly regarding their mental state and their attitude to conversation, it's stated that they tend to be anxious and prefer to be left alone — wouldn't this lead to shorter, snippier and perhaps more chiding responses, or even a lack of responses at all? Just because the therapists were able to persuade them doesn't mean the persuasion was wholly effective.

Overall thoughts:

I think there is potential for a far more original idea, and would encourage you to seek out ways of doing so. Work on your dialogue as well — shorter responses can convey just as much information or very little information, and the latter is handy if you want to build a sense of intrigue from the reader.

Neutral-voting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's just shy of up-vote territory for me - the dialogue is a bit croaky, for example.

If this gets deleted, or if you're interested anyway, I'd be willing to help you change it. Drop me a PM if so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for your comment! I can understand the criticism for the interview, but I thought the whole idea of someone like this would sort of run into a tirade about his life due to his issues. So it was more along the lines of 'letting the scp' talk/ramble.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea of it, the writing is easy to read and understand.

I think the interview is a bit out of place, however. I think you went into a bit too much detail after the "Did you isolate yourself from others" question. I don't think it'd want to share its entire life story to a interviewer. I don't think you should go into a lot of detail about his past or personality- You could improve this by taking out details or sentences that are unnecessary, or you wouldn't share if you were it.

I fixed some grammar issues in the dialogue, but I probably missed some. Other than that, the idea is original and this wasn't hard to read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the concept, but I very much dislike the character and the interview. It devolves into the character simply telling the story of their life, and the story doesn't feel real to me - no subtlety at all. Overall -1, could probably be improved a lot however.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are a few grammatical errors here and there (example, you're and your confusion), but other than that I think this is a very interesting concept and I like where its going. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for your comment!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This feels like the Series 3 version of Vermin God and I love it a lot.

I'm also glad you took all the advice you got for the rewrite and I hope this gets upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have the feeling I've seen this somewhere before, but I upvoted primarily because of this:

and some birds smashed through my window, and trying to shove their worms down my throat.

i'm dying

If this gets downvoted to three stories below the netherworld, my advice to you would be to flesh out the concept a little more, maybe add something extra and original. I didn't notice any overt tone issues, and you're a great writer, so that's probably the main thing you should have a look at.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Credits to Westydude for helping me with the dialogue (I'm not the best at it.)
